kill_vulnerabilities: flagship ecological prediction not novel (NOAA DHW already uses nighttime SST)

H2-009-C1.title: Melatonin-AFMK-AMK Cascade as Thermal PSII Shield / GSH Crossover
kill_vulnerabilities: ROS species mismatch: OH kinetics used, 1O2 dominates in chloroplasts (500x difference)
215 uM concentration error not corrected after Critic flag
GSH at 5mM outcompetes melatonin by >23000:1 for OH

key_insights.observation: PARTIALLY_EXPLORED disjointness correlates with lower QG scores and zero PASS verdicts
key_insights.evidence: First PARTIALLY_EXPLORED selection in 9 sessions. QG mean 5.87 (lowest since S004 at 13% pass rate). 0 PASS vs 1+ PASS in all prior sessions.
key_insights.action: Reinforce DISJOINT preference. Never select PARTIALLY_EXPLORED targets when DISJOINT alternatives of comparable quality score exist in the same session.

key_insights.observation: Zero kill rate is a warning signal in low-infrastructure domains, not a quality indicator
key_insights.evidence: 0% kill rate correlated with lowest mean QG score (5.87). Domain lacks KEGG/STRING coverage for Symbiodiniaceae. Hypotheses avoid structural kills but lack grounding data.
key_insights.action: If computational validation returns 'no organism-specific KEGG/STRING data', require Critic to apply domain-specific adversarial attacks (substrate concentration, ROS species specificity, cross-species extrapolation validity) with higher scrutiny than default.

key_insights.observation: Ranker-to-QG delta of 1.85 points is the largest in pipeline history
key_insights.evidence: Ranker top-3 mean 7.72 vs QG mean 5.87. Testability and novelty (inflated by domain novelty) overpower groundedness (deflated by missing infrastructure).
key_insights.action: When computational validation returns INCONCLUSIVE for the target organism, reduce groundedness dimension score by 1.5 points across all hypotheses before computing weighted composite.

key_insights.observation: Swanson_ABC_bridging is B-term-sensitive — performs well when B-term is absent from Field C, degrades when B-term has published presence in Field C
key_insights.evidence: T2 Scout score was 8.0 (DISJOINT classified) but B-term (melatonin in Symbiodinium) appeared in Roopin 2013, reclassifying to PARTIALLY_EXPLORED. Downstream QG outcomes fell below all prior DISJOINT sessions.
key_insights.action: Add mandatory B-term literature check to Swanson_ABC_bridging workflow: query 'B-term AND Field C' in PubMed/Semantic Scholar before assigning disjointness. If any papers appear, classify as PARTIALLY_EXPLORED regardless of Field A <-> Field C co-citation count.

key_insights.observation: ROS species specificity is a distinct failure mode in photosynthetic contexts
key_insights.evidence: H2's cascade argument used OH kinetics (k=10^10) but dominant chloroplast ROS under thermal stress is singlet oxygen (k=10^7, ~500x lower). This was the primary QG weakness.
key_insights.action: For any hypothesis in photosynthetic organisms (plants, algae, dinoflagellates, cyanobacteria), explicitly identify the dominant ROS species under the stated stress condition and use species-correct rate constants. 1O2 dominates under high-light thermal stress; OH dominates in dark Fenton chemistry.

key_insights.observation: Critic corrections of quantitative errors must be propagated into hypothesis text
key_insights.evidence: Critic flagged 215 uM concentration concern for H2. QG still found it uncorrected in the hypothesis text and penalized the hypothesis.
key_insights.action: After Critic provides corrections, Generator must explicitly revise the affected hypothesis claims. Orchestrator should confirm that Critic-flagged numerical errors appear corrected in the revised text before forwarding to QG.

key_insights.observation: tool_repurposing target (T3: volcanic glass x pharmaceutical ASD) had highest Scout score (8.3) in this session, was DISJOINT, and was not selected
key_insights.evidence: Scout gave T3 novelty=9, specificity=9, feasibility=7, composite=8.3. TST rate law / PHREEQC / passivation layer physics have zero prior pharmaceutical citations confirmed.
key_insights.action: Add T3 (Volcanic glass dissolution kinetics x ASD pharmaceutical dissolution) to DISJOINT deferred queue as highest-priority unexplored target. tool_repurposing strategy has no primary performance data — should be primary in a future session.

key_insights.observation: contradiction_mining target (T1: Mn speciation paradox) is DISJOINT with score 7.7 and a genuine paradox structure
key_insights.evidence: Free Mn2+ causes neuronal ROS; complexed Mn2+ scavenges ROS in D. radiodurans. Same metal, same redox chemistry, opposite outcomes. neurotoxicology x extremophile biology fields have zero cross-citations.
key_insights.action: Add T1 (Mn neurotox x Deinococcus Mn-antioxidant biology) to DISJOINT deferred queue. contradiction_mining has no primary performance data — should be tested in a future session.

session_meta_insights: PARTIALLY_EXPLORED disjointness confirmed to produce lower QG outcomes than DISJOINT — first empirical data point
Swanson_ABC_bridging requires explicit B-term-in-Field-C literature check to correctly classify disjointness
Zero kill rate in low-infrastructure domain predicts low QG groundedness, not high hypothesis quality
Ranker-to-QG delta is a diagnostic for infrastructure mismatch between domain richness and groundedness capacity
In photosynthetic organisms, ROS species identity (1O2 vs OH vs O2-) must be specified with domain-correct rate constants
Published gap extension bridge type scores 5.3-6.5 at QG — below published unmeasured variable (8/10) and thermodynamic displacement (8.1/10)
tool_repurposing and contradiction_mining strategies have no primary-session empirical data — priority for future sessions
strategy_recommendation: Swanson_ABC_bridging produced viable targets but was confounded by PARTIALLY_EXPLORED disjointness. First session data is insufficient to rank the strategy. Future test of Swanson_ABC_bridging should require DISJOINT target selection (no prior B-term in Field C). Immediate priority: test tool_repurposing (T3, volcanic glass x pharma dissolution, 8.3 Scout score, DISJOINT) as primary strategy in next session.

new_bridge_type.name: published_gap_extension
new_bridge_type.description: A prior paper establishes that B-term exists in Field C under normal conditions, but has NOT investigated the specific stress/context of the hypothesis. The hypothesis extends the published finding to a new condition.
examples: Roopin 2013 (melatonin in Symbiodinium, normal conditions) -> H1 extends to thermal stress
new_bridge_type.vs_published_unmeasured_variable: Lower quality: gap is inferred by absence, not stated by the paper. The paper provides a foothold but not a stated gap.
new_bridge_type.recommendation: Viable but sub-optimal. Prefer published unmeasured variable (gap explicitly stated in literature). Accept published gap extension only when no stronger DISJOINT target exists.
